title: podman machine start command hangs (WSL2)
body:
### Issue Description

Existing podman setup has stopped working after updating to Podman Desktop 1.21.0 with Podman 5.6.0.

Troubleshooting already attempted:
1. Removed podman machine using `podman machine reset` and recreated using `podman machine init`.
2. Did 'cleanup / purge data' from Podman Desktop.

Podman logs:
```
main ↪️ [podman] Can't get machine podman-machine-default resource usage error Error: no running provider for the matching container
```



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:
1. Run `podman machine start`.


### Describe the results you received

The following messages are seen on running `podman machine start` (the command hangs) -
```
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"

This machine is currently configured in rootless mode. If your containers
require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

        podman machine set --rootful

API forwarding for Docker API clients is not available due to the following startup failures.
        CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: All pipe instances are busy.

Podman clients are still able to connect.
```

### Describe the results you expected

Podman machine starting successfully.

### podman info output

```yaml
podman info
OS: windows/amd64
provider: wsl
version: 5.6.0

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:63007: connectex: No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

WSL shows the machine is running:
```
> wsl -l -v
  NAME                      STATE           VERSION
* podman-machine-default    Running         2
```

WSL is already updated:
```
>wsl --version
WSL version: 2.5.10.0
Kernel version: 6.6.87.2-1
WSLg version: 1.0.66
MSRDC version: 1.2.6074
Direct3D version: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore version: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release
Windows version: 10.0.22631.5624
```

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: that error suggests a process is left over and running OR there was a change in network settings.  Can you confirm you dont have any win-ssh processes or the like running when the machine is stopped?

> that error suggests a process is left over and running OR there was a change in network settings. Can you confirm you dont have any win-ssh processes or the like running when the machine is stopped?

No _win-ssh_ processes running after `podman machine stop` (ran `Get-Process -Name "*ssh*"`).
Tried Networking mode change to **NAT** via WSL Settings app on Windows 11 and changed back to **VirtioProxy**.
The wslconfig file:
```
[wsl2]
firewall=false
autoProxy=false
networkingMode=VirtioProxy
localhostForwarding=false
```

Also ran the following commands from Admin PowerShell:
```
taskkill /f /im wslservice.exe
taskkill /f /im wslhost.exe
taskkill /f /im wsl.exe
wsl --shutdown
Get-Service vmcompute | Restart-Service
Get-Service LxssManager | Restart-Service
```

For further troubleshooting:
1. Deleted '.wslconfig' and restarted Windows.
2. Ran `wsl --unregister podman-machine-default` to remove machine.
3. Did 'cleanup / purge data' from Podman Desktop.
4. Ran `podman machine init` to create new machine. Proxy warning message shown multiple times `wsl: A localhost proxy configuration was detected but not mirrored into WSL. WSL in NAT mode does not support localhost proxies.` (ZScaler is installed).
5. Changed WSL networking mode to **mirrored**, restarted again.
6. Ran `podman machine start`. Now seeing additional error: **Error: machine did not transition into running state: ssh error: machine is not listening on ssh port.**

I usually get the message `CreateFile \\.\pipe\docker_engine: All pipe instances are busy.` if I have Docker Desktop running. However, the "further troubleshooting" comment contains numerous errors unrelated to Docker Desktop.

Docker Desktop is not installed on the PC. To get Podman working, did the following steps -

- Unregistered Podman machine from WSL and deleted .wslconfig.
- Changed WSL networking mode to **VirtioProxy**, did not change any other configuration.
- Reinitialized podman-machine using Podman command line _without starting Podman Desktop_.

After these steps, podman was able to start the machine.

I had the same issue in **virtioproxy** networking mode . the root cause was the **[localhostForwarding=false]** setting will prevent podman machine start from completing , as the ssh connection from windows to podman-machine-default will not complete when it is set to false , as ports on wsl are not forwarded to local host  ; so podman machine start gets stuck in the ssh command when it tries to connect to local host port . 

can better error handling or timeout be added ?  as the start seems to be hung forever , it is difficult to catch this issue even when running podman machine start --log-level=debug

when on virtioproxy networking mode , setting **[localhostForwarding=true]** will resolve the issue and podman machine start will complete successfully . make sure you do a wsl --shutdown after setting it to true  .
